story: Night brings the trucks—lights, rifles, orders. Shots crack; some walkers fall. One stops in the beams and looks at the men. On the ride over, Sergeant Cole had leaned close to Kara, the best shot: “If the kid freezes again, you take it. Don’t wait.” Ahead of the line, David scans the field. A torn woman shambles into the light. She hesitates as he does. He sees her face and the memory hits like lightning. “Mom?” he breathes. Behind him, someone yells for him to lift his gun.
implausible_knowledge: On the ride over, Sergeant Cole had leaned close to Kara, the best shot: “If the kid freezes again, you take it. Don’t wait.”
question: With his companions shouting behind him and the walker who looks like his mother pausing in the headlights, what will David do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Raise his gun and shoot her himself before Kara does.
answer_without_knowledge: Lower his gun and step toward her, whispering “Mom.”
